decisions.briefing: Markets drifted sideways today with both NASDAQ and S&P 500 up just 0.2%, offering no compelling catalyst to deploy capital. Cash sits at $444.94 — just under the $500 threshold — so no new positions are initiated, and all six current holdings remain within normal drawdown ranges with no stop-losses triggered. AAOI is the only position showing a paper loss (-3.6% from avg), but it retains strong momentum signals at score 95 and is well above its -7% stop-loss floor.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities edged marginally higher on May 11, with both the S&P 500 and NASDAQ gaining roughly 0.2% in a low-conviction session. Traders appeared to be in a wait-and-see posture ahead of upcoming economic data, with no major macro catalysts driving directional moves. Sector rotation was muted, and breadth was roughly flat, suggesting institutional players were neither adding nor reducing risk aggressively.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市5月11日小幅收高，標普500與納斯達克指數均上漲約0.2%，整體走勢方向感不明。市場參與者普遍採觀望態度，靜待後續重要經濟數據出爐，當日缺乏明顯宏觀催化劑。板塊輪動表現平淡，市場廣度近乎持平，顯示機構投資者既未積極加倉，亦未大幅減碼。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance penalty from holding uninvested cash — idle money earns little while the market moves up. But the flip side is opportunity cost: deploying cash into weak setups just to stay 'fully invested' can erode returns more than the drag itself. Today's decision to hold $444 in cash rather than chase a marginal position illustrates disciplined capital allocation — waiting for the right entry beats forcing a trade.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指持有未投資現金所帶來的績效損失——閒置資金幾乎沒有回報，而市場卻可能持續上漲。然而，另一面是「機會成本」：僅為保持「滿倉」而倉促入市，往往比持有現金的損失更大。今日選擇保留444美元現金、不強行建倉的決定，正體現了嚴謹的資本配置原則——等待合適的進場時機，遠比強迫交易來得明智。
